liquidityNotes: GFI token liquidity should be evaluated through secondary-market trading, not redemption against private-credit collateral. DeFiLlama snapshot shows GFI liquidity concentrated in Uniswap V3 with modest DEX liquidity and higher CEX volume. Goldfinch Prime has separate product-level withdrawal mechanics, including quarterly withdrawal processing referenced in official FAQ. Product-level Goldfinch Prime liquidity must not be used as native GFI redemption liquidity.

_meta.scope: GFI token liquidity layer with Goldfinch Prime liquidity kept only as related product context.

goldfinchPrime.lockup: Official FAQ states no lockup for Goldfinch Prime, but withdrawals are processed quarterly and are subject to product liquidity mechanics.
goldfinchPrime.feeContext: Official FAQ references a 0.5% withdrawal fee for Goldfinch Prime.

_meta.liquidityScoreRationale: Score 45 is conservative. GFI has public secondary-market trading and measurable holders, but onchain DEX liquidity appears modest, bid-ask spread was not verified, redemption is not native to GFI, and product-level withdrawal mechanics do not improve token redemption liquidity.
dataGaps: Specific CoinGecko/CoinMarketCap exchange-level order book depth not captured.
Bid-ask spread not verified from exchange order books.
Full DEX pair list and pair-specific URLs should be captured from GeckoTerminal or DEXScreener before production scoring.
Holder concentration and top-wallet liquidity risk require deeper Etherscan analysis.
